title: cytinus hypocistis subsp. kermesinus
intro: Cytinus hypocistis subsp. kermesinus, commonly known as the red broom or Mediterranean broom, is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching to the roots of other plants. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cytinus hypocistis subsp. kermesinus include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, which are often used in traditional herbal remedies to treat various health conditions.
